title: ServerRequest
description: Request initiated from the server and sent to the client.

oneOf.description: NEW APIs Sent when approval is requested for a specific command execution. This request is used for Turns started via turn/start.
oneOf.title: Item/commandExecution/requestApprovalRequest

oneOf.description: Sent when approval is requested for a specific file change. This request is used for Turns started via turn/start.
oneOf.title: Item/fileChange/requestApprovalRequest

oneOf.description: EXPERIMENTAL - Request input from the user for a tool call.
oneOf.title: Item/tool/requestUserInputRequest

oneOf.description: Request input for an MCP server elicitation.
oneOf.title: McpServer/elicitation/requestRequest

oneOf.description: Request approval for additional permissions from the user.
oneOf.title: Item/permissions/requestApprovalRequest

oneOf.description: Execute a dynamic tool call on the client.
oneOf.title: Item/tool/callRequest

oneOf.description: Generate a fresh upstream attestation result on demand.
oneOf.title: Attestation/generateRequest

oneOf.description: Read the current time from an external clock owned by the client.
oneOf.title: CurrentTime/readRequest

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED APIs below Request to approve a patch. This request is used for Turns started via the legacy APIs (i.e. SendUserTurn, SendUserMessage).
oneOf.title: ApplyPatchApprovalRequest

oneOf.description: Request to exec a command. This request is used for Turns started via the legacy APIs (i.e. SendUserTurn, SendUserMessage).
oneOf.title: ExecCommandApprovalRequest

AbsolutePathBuf.description:
A path that is guaranteed to be absolute and normalized (though it is not guaranteed to be canonicalized or exist on the filesystem).

IMPORTANT: When deserializing an `AbsolutePathBuf`, a base path must be set using [AbsolutePathBufGuard::new]. If no base path is set, the deserialization will fail unless the path being deserialized is already absolute.

read.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

write.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

network.description: Partial overlay used for per-command permission requests.

callId.description: Use to correlate this with [codex_protocol::protocol::PatchApplyBeginEvent] and [codex_protocol::protocol::PatchApplyEndEvent].

grantRoot.description: When set, the agent is asking the user to allow writes under this root for the remainder of the session (unclear if this is honored today).

reason.description: Optional explanatory reason (e.g. request for extra write access).

previousAccountId.description:
Workspace/account identifier that Codex was previously using.

Clients that manage multiple accounts/workspaces can use this as a hint to refresh the token for the correct workspace.

This may be `null` when the prior auth state did not include a workspace identifier (`chatgpt_account_id`).

oneOf.description: Codex attempted a backend request and received `401 Unauthorized`.
User approved the command and future prompts in the same session-scoped approval cache should run without prompting.
User approved the command, and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy amendment so future matching commands can run without prompting.
oneOf.title: AcceptWithExecpolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User chose a persistent network policy rule (allow/deny) for this host.
oneOf.title: ApplyNetworkPolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User denied the command. The agent will continue the turn.
User denied the command. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

additionalPermissions.description: Optional additional permissions requested for this command.

approvalId.description:
Unique identifier for this specific approval callback.

For regular shell/unified_exec approvals, this is null.

For zsh-exec-bridge subcommand approvals, multiple callbacks can belong to one parent `itemId`, so `approvalId` is a distinct opaque callback id (a UUID) used to disambiguate routing.

availableDecisions.description: Ordered list of decisions the client may present for this prompt.

commandActions.description: Best-effort parsed command actions for friendly display.

environmentId.description: Environment in which the command will run.

networkApprovalContext.description: Optional context for a managed-network approval prompt.

proposedExecpolicyAmendment.description: Optional proposed execpolicy amendment to allow similar commands without prompting.

proposedNetworkPolicyAmendments.description: Optional proposed network policy amendments (allow/deny host) for future requests.

reason.description: Optional explanatory reason (e.g. request for network access).

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this approval request started.

approvalId.description: Identifier for this specific approval callback.

callId.description: Use to correlate this with [codex_protocol::protocol::ExecCommandBeginEvent] and [codex_protocol::protocol::ExecCommandEndEvent].

grantRoot.description: [UNSTABLE] When set, the agent is asking the user to allow writes under this root for the remainder of the session (unclear if this is honored today).

reason.description: Optional explanatory reason (e.g. request for extra write access).

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this approval request started.

McpElicitationSchema.description:
Typed form schema for MCP `elicitation/create` requests.

This matches the `requestedSchema` shape from the MCP 2025-11-25 `ElicitRequestFormParams` schema.

turnId.description:
Active Codex turn when this elicitation was observed, if app-server could correlate one.

This is nullable because MCP models elicitation as a standalone server-to-client request identified by the MCP server request id. It may be triggered during a turn, but turn context is app-server correlation rather than part of the protocol identity of the elicitation itself.

path.description: (Best effort) Path to the file being read by the command. When possible, this is an absolute path, though when relative, it should be resolved against the `cwd`` that will be used to run the command to derive the absolute path.

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this approval request started.

ToolRequestUserInputOption.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Defines a single selectable option for request_user_input.

ToolRequestUserInputParams.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Params sent with a request_user_input event.

ToolRequestUserInputQuestion.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Represents one request_user_input question and its required options.
